description: Bidirectional sync tool for Pi and Claude Code session history across machines using path canonicalization and Git as storage/transport backend

userStories.title: Project scaffolding, error types, and CLI skeleton
userStories.description: Set up the Rust project foundation: Cargo.toml with all dependencies (clap, serde, serde_json, toml, git2, chrono, dirs, uuid, rand, tracing, thiserror, anyhow), module directory structure with mod.rs files for all modules (cli, config, canon, merge, git, agents, scheduler, errors, scan), main.rs with clap CLI skeleton defining all commands (init, import, sync, push, pull, status, errors, config, schedule) with handler stubs returning Ok, and thiserror error types covering all error categories (push_conflict, malformed_line, prefix_mismatch, canonicalization_error, git_error, io_error, disk_full).
acceptanceCriteria: Cargo.toml compiles with all listed dependencies
All module directories exist with mod.rs files
main.rs parses all CLI commands via clap (handler stubs print 'not implemented' and return Ok)
Error types defined with thiserror for all 7 categories from §11.1
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes (no tests yet is acceptable)
cargo fmt --check passes

userStories.title: Config schema, loading, validation, and machine name generator
userStories.description: Implement the full configuration system per §8. Define serde structs for the complete config.toml schema (general, notifications, storage, canonicalization, agents, sync sections). Implement TOML loading with sensible defaults, environment variable overrides (CHRONICLE_REPO_PATH, CHRONICLE_REMOTE_URL, CHRONICLE_SYNC_INTERVAL), CLI flag overrides, and the precedence chain (CLI > env > file > defaults). Use XDG-compliant path resolution via the dirs crate. Implement the adjective-animal machine name generator (§8.4) with a curated word list.
acceptanceCriteria: Config struct covers all fields from §8.2
Loading from TOML file works with all sections
Missing config file produces sensible defaults
Environment variables override config file values
Precedence chain: CLI > env > file > defaults verified by unit tests
XDG_CONFIG_HOME respected for config path
Machine name generator produces adjective-animal names (e.g., cheerful-chinchilla)
Unit tests for config loading, defaults, env overrides, and name generation
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Agent directory encoding (Pi and Claude)
userStories.description: Implement Pi and Claude agent-specific directory encoding and decoding per §4.4. Pi uses --encoded-- format (leading / stripped, all / replaced with -). Claude uses -encoded format (leading / stripped, all / and . replaced with -). Both must support round-trip encode/decode. Include schema knowledge for each agent's session directory structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Pi encode: /Users/bradmatic/Dev/foo → --Users-bradmatic-Dev-foo--
Pi decode: --Users-bradmatic-Dev-foo-- → /Users/bradmatic/Dev/foo
Claude encode: /Users/bradmatic/Dev/foo → -Users-bradmatic-Dev-foo
Claude decode: -Users-bradmatic-Dev-foo → /Users/bradmatic/Dev/foo
Round-trip tests pass for both agents with various path inputs
Edge cases handled: paths with dots (Claude), deeply nested paths
Agent trait or module exposes session_dir, encode_dir, decode_dir
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Token registry and L1 path canonicalization
userStories.description: Implement the token registry (§4.1, §4.6) managing SYNC_HOME and custom tokens. Implement L1 path canonicalization (§4.4) which transforms file and directory paths by replacing the encoded home path prefix with the token form. Token ordering: custom tokens applied after SYNC_HOME during canonicalization, before SYNC_HOME during de-canonicalization. Path-boundary matching must avoid partial matches (e.g., /Users/bradmatic2 must NOT match /Users/bradmatic).
acceptanceCriteria: Token registry holds SYNC_HOME and accepts custom tokens from config
L1 canonicalization replaces home path prefix in directory names for both Pi and Claude encodings
L1 de-canonicalization reverses the transform with the local machine's home path
Custom tokens applied in correct order (after SYNC_HOME for canon, before for decanon)
Path-boundary matching: /Users/bradmatic2 is NOT matched when home is /Users/bradmatic
Unit tests for token ordering, boundary matching, and round-trip
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: L2 field canonicalization, L3 freeform, and de-canonicalization
userStories.description: Implement L2 structured field canonicalization (§4.3): walk whitelisted JSON field paths (cwd, path, file_path, message.cwd, arguments.path, arguments.file_path, arguments.command) and replace values starting with $HOME. Implement L3 freeform text canonicalization (§4.2) as opt-in with startup warning. Implement full de-canonicalization reversing L1, L2, and L3 transforms. Verify the round-trip invariant (§4.7): decanon(canon(content, A), A) == content.
acceptanceCriteria: L2 canonicalizes only whitelisted JSON field paths
Only string values starting with $HOME/ or equal to $HOME are replaced
Non-path strings, UUIDs, and entry IDs are never touched
L3 scans all string content when level=3, emits startup warning
De-canonicalization reverses L1+L2+L3 with local home path
Round-trip invariant holds: decanon(canon(x, A), A) == x for all levels
Custom token nesting works (SYNC_PROJECTS under SYNC_HOME)
Unit tests for each level, field whitelist, round-trip, and edge cases
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: JSONL entry parsing and grow-only set merge
userStories.description: Implement JSONL entry parsing (§5.1): parse each line as a JSON object, extract composite identity key (type + id), detect session headers (type=session). Implement the grow-only set merge algorithm (§5.2): parse both files into entry sets, compute union, order results (header first, then by timestamp ascending, stable sort with remote-first determinism). Handle malformed lines (§5.5): skip unparseable lines, log warning with file path and line number, continue processing.
acceptanceCriteria: Entry identity correctly uses (type, id) composite key
Session header identified by type=session (exactly one per file)
Set-union merge produces union of both entry sets
Merged output: header first, then entries sorted by timestamp ascending
Stable sort: identical timestamps preserve order (remote entries first)
Malformed lines are skipped with warning (file path, line number, snippet)
Merge of two empty files produces empty output
Merge with one-sided input (new file) returns that file's entries
Unit tests for all merge scenarios from §5.3
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Merge prefix verification and conflict resolution
userStories.description: Implement prefix verification (§5.4): before merging, verify that common entries (present in both versions) are byte-identical after canonicalization. If they differ, log a detailed warning identifying the file and mismatched entries, proceed with remote-version-wins, and record the incident in the error ring buffer. Integrate with the error ring buffer interface (can use a trait/callback for now if ring buffer isn't built yet).
acceptanceCriteria: Common entries (same type+id in both files) are verified byte-identical
Mismatched common entries: detailed warning logged with file path and entry details
Remote-version-wins applied for conflicting entries
Incident recorded via error reporting interface (trait or callback)
Non-conflicting entries unaffected by verification
Unit tests: identical prefix passes, divergent prefix triggers warning and uses remote
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Error ring buffer
userStories.description: Implement the 30-entry error ring buffer (§11.1) stored as JSONL at ~/.local/share/chronicle/errors.jsonl. Each entry includes timestamp, severity, category, file, message, and detail fields. Support all error categories: push_conflict, malformed_line, prefix_mismatch, canonicalization_error, git_error, io_error, disk_full. Implement append (with rotation at 30 entries), read (with optional limit), and clear operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Ring buffer stored at XDG-compliant path (~/.local/share/chronicle/errors.jsonl)
Each entry contains: timestamp, severity, category, file, message, detail
Buffer rotates at 30 entries (oldest dropped)
All 7 error categories supported
Read with optional --limit N returns last N entries
Append is atomic (no partial writes on crash)
Unit tests for append, rotation, read with limit, and all categories
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: File change scanner
userStories.description: Implement file change detection (§14.1) using mtime and file size comparison against a cached state. Maintain state.json at ~/.local/share/chronicle/state.json tracking local_mtime, local_size, last_synced_size, and local_path per file. On first run (no cache), all files are considered changed. Support scanning configured agent session directories for .jsonl files, respecting the follow_symlinks config option (§12).
acceptanceCriteria: Scanner detects new files (not in cache)
Scanner detects modified files (mtime or size changed)
Scanner ignores unchanged files (mtime and size match cache)
state.json persisted at XDG-compliant path
First run with no cache treats all files as changed
Symlinks refused when follow_symlinks=false (error logged)
Symlinks followed when follow_symlinks=true (warning logged)
Unit tests for new/modified/unchanged detection and symlink policy
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Git repo management (init and working tree)
userStories.description: Implement git repo management via git2 (§6.1–6.3). Support initializing a new bare repo at the configured repo_path, setting up the working tree with the canonical directory structure (pi/sessions/, claude/projects/, .chronicle/), and managing the manifest.json metadata file. Handle the case where the repo already exists (open instead of re-init).
acceptanceCriteria: Git repo initialized at configured repo_path via git2
Repo opened without error if it already exists
Working tree has canonical structure: pi/sessions/, claude/projects/, .chronicle/
manifest.json created with version, machines map
Remote URL configured on the repo
Unit tests for init, open-existing, and directory structure
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Git fetch/push with retry and backoff
userStories.description: Implement git fetch and push operations via git2 (§6.5). Push includes retry logic with exponential backoff: 3 retries at 0s, 5s, 25s intervals. On push rejection (remote advanced), retry the full fetch→merge→commit→push cycle. After 3 failures, log error to the ring buffer and exit gracefully. Handle network failures (§11.3): log error, skip cycle, let cron retry.
acceptanceCriteria: git fetch from configured remote via git2
git push to configured remote via git2
Push retry with exponential backoff: 0s, 5s, 25s (3 max retries)
Push rejection triggers fetch→re-merge→commit→push retry
After 3 failures: error logged to ring buffer, exits gracefully
Network failure: logged, cycle skipped (no immediate retry)
Unit tests for retry logic and backoff timing
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Git commit formatting and staging
userStories.description: Implement git staging and commit formatting via git2 (§6.4). Support two commit formats: sync commits (sync: machine @ timestamp, with +N/~N file counts per agent) and import commits (import: agent sessions (machine), with file count). Use the machine name from config as the git committer name with chronicle@local as email.
acceptanceCriteria: Files staged via git2 add
Sync commit format: 'sync: machine @ ISO-timestamp' with +N/~N body
Import commit format: 'import: agent sessions (machine)' with file count body
Committer name = machine_name from config, email = chronicle@local
No commit created if no staged changes (idempotent)
Unit tests for commit message formatting
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: CLI init command
userStories.description: Implement the chronicle init command (§9.1). Creates ~/.config/chronicle/config.toml with defaults, generates a machine name via the adjective-animal generator, initializes the local git repo at repo_path, and prompts for remote_url if not provided via flag. Does NOT import existing files.
acceptanceCriteria: Config file created at XDG-compliant path with all default values
Machine name auto-generated and written to config
Git repo initialized at configured repo_path
User prompted for remote_url if not provided via --remote flag
Idempotent: re-running init on existing setup does not corrupt state
Prints confirmation with machine name and repo path
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: CLI import command
userStories.description: Implement the chronicle import command (§6.6, §9.1). Performs a one-time bulk import: scan all configured agent session directories for .jsonl files, canonicalize each file, commit one commit per agent session directory for atomicity, and push all commits. Support --agent pi|claude|all filter and --dry-run mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Scans configured agent session directories for .jsonl files
Each file canonicalized (L1+L2) and written to repo working tree
One commit per session directory (not per file, not one giant commit)
Import commit message format per §6.4
--agent flag filters to pi, claude, or all (default: all)
--dry-run shows what would be imported without writing
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: CLI push and pull commands
userStories.description: Implement chronicle push and chronicle pull commands (§9.1). Push: canonicalize local changes, commit, push to remote (outgoing only, no pull or de-canonicalization). Pull: fetch from remote, merge at entry level, de-canonicalize, write to local agent directories (incoming only, no push). Both support --dry-run.
acceptanceCriteria: Push: scans local changes, canonicalizes, commits, pushes to remote
Push does NOT fetch or de-canonicalize
Pull: fetches from remote, merges conflicts at JSONL entry level, de-canonicalizes, writes locally
Pull does NOT push local changes
Both support --dry-run flag
File permissions preserved per §11.5
Errors logged to ring buffer
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Partial history materialization
userStories.description: Implement partial history materialization (§7). When history_mode=partial, only the N most recent session files per directory are de-canonicalized and written locally during incoming sync. 'Most recent' determined by filename timestamp (Pi) or earliest entry timestamp (Claude). Files outside the window are not deleted if they already exist. Outgoing sync is unaffected by partial setting.
acceptanceCriteria: When history_mode=full, all files materialized
When history_mode=partial, only partial_max_count most recent files per directory materialized
Pi: recency from filename timestamp
Claude: recency from earliest entry timestamp in file
Existing local files outside the window are NOT deleted
Outgoing sync always pushes all local files regardless of partial setting
Unit tests for recency ordering and partial filtering
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: CLI sync command
userStories.description: Implement the chronicle sync command (§9.1, §14) — the full bidirectional sync cycle that cron invokes. Orchestrates: load config → validate repo/remote → outgoing phase (scan, canonicalize, merge, commit) → git exchange (fetch, merge remote, push with retry) → incoming phase (de-canonicalize, partial filter, write local, preserve permissions) → update state cache and manifest.json. Support --dry-run and --quiet flags.
acceptanceCriteria: Full sync cycle executes all phases from §14 in order
Outgoing: changed files detected via scanner, canonicalized, merged if needed, committed
Git exchange: fetch, merge remote changes at entry level, push with retry
Incoming: de-canonicalize, apply partial history filter, write to local dirs
State cache (state.json) updated after successful sync
manifest.json updated with last_sync timestamp
--dry-run shows all phases without writing
--quiet suppresses non-error output
Idempotent: running sync with no changes creates no commits or file writes
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: CLI status, errors, and config commands
userStories.description: Implement three display/utility CLI commands. Status (§9.1): show last sync time, pending local changes, remote changes (requires fetch), machine name, and configured agents. Errors (§9.1): display the error ring buffer (last 30 by default, --limit N). Config (§9.1): view full config, view single key, set key+value, special machine-name key.
acceptanceCriteria: chronicle status shows last sync time, pending changes, machine name, agent status
chronicle errors displays ring buffer entries (default 30, --limit N)
chronicle config prints full config
chronicle config <key> prints single value
chronicle config <key> <value> sets value and writes config
chronicle config machine-name shows/sets machine identity
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Scheduler (cron management)
userStories.description: Implement chronicle schedule install/uninstall/status commands (§10). Install writes @reboot and interval crontab entries tagged with # chronicle-sync marker. Uninstall removes entries by marker. Status reports installed state, interval, and binary path. Map sync_interval config to cron expressions per §10.2 (1m, 5m, 10m, 15m, 30m, 1h). Non-standard intervals rounded down with warning.
acceptanceCriteria: schedule install writes @reboot + interval entries to crontab
Entries tagged with # chronicle-sync comment marker
Existing entries updated in place (not duplicated)
schedule uninstall removes all # chronicle-sync entries
Empty crontab after removal triggers crontab -r
schedule status reports: installed (yes/no), interval, binary path
Interval mapping: 1m, 5m, 10m, 15m, 30m, 1h to correct cron expressions
Non-standard intervals (e.g., 7m) rounded down with warning
Auto-detects chronicle binary path
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Property-based tests
userStories.description: Implement property-based tests using proptest (§15.3). Cover: merge commutativity (merge(A,B) == merge(B,A)), merge associativity (merge(merge(A,B),C) == merge(A,merge(B,C))), merge idempotency (merge(A,A) == A), canonicalization round-trip (decanon(canon(x)) == x for all levels and any home path), and merge superset (entries(merge(A,B)) ⊇ entries(A) ∪ entries(B)).
acceptanceCriteria: proptest strategy generates valid JSONL entry sets
Merge commutativity property holds for generated inputs
Merge associativity property holds for generated inputs
Merge idempotency property holds for generated inputs
Canon round-trip property holds for all levels (L1, L2, L3) with random home paths
Merge superset property: merged result contains all entries from both inputs
Tests run via cargo test (proptest integrated)
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Integration tests
userStories.description: Implement integration tests (§15.2) in tests/ directory. Cover key scenarios: two-machine basic sync, concurrent append to different files, concurrent append to same file, canonicalization round-trip across machines, partial history materialization, network failure during push/fetch, disk full during writes, malformed JSONL handling, push conflict exhaustion, new machine bootstrap, symlink policy, L3 freeform canonicalization, custom tokens, import batching, and idempotent sync.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests in tests/ directory
Two-machine sync: A creates session, syncs; B syncs, sees session
Concurrent append (different files): both machines get all entries
Concurrent append (same file): merged result contains union
Canon round-trip: file with home paths survives A→canon→store→decanon→B
Partial history: 200 sessions, partial_max_count=50, only 50 materialized
Malformed JSONL: corrupted line skipped, valid lines preserved
Import batching: 50 files across 5 dirs = 5 commits
Idempotent sync: no changes → no commits or file writes
All tests pass via cargo test
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
